task.title: Distinguish record literals from block openers when joining braces
task.summary: The formatter is treating some record literals as though they were control flow or closure blocks.
task.problem: Brace rejoining is too syntax-blind. A returned or assigned record literal can be attached as if it were the start of a block, which produces malformed or misleading layouts around let bindings and returned records.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Record literals after let, return, or expression positions are formatted as records, not as block openers.
Control-flow and closure block opener rejoining still works where appropriate.
Regression tests cover returned records and records following let bindings inside closures.
